content.problemStatement:
Suppose the population is divided into the age groups [1,10],[11,20],[21,30],…,[91,∞)[1, 10], [11, 20], [21, 30], \ldots, [91, \infty)[1,10],[11,20],[21,30],…,[91,∞). The age groups are numbered from 111 to 101010 and there are XiX_iXi​ people in age group iii.

The COVID vaccine drive has started and people will be vaccinated in the decreasing order of their age groups. Suppose PPP people are vaccinated per day and if less than PPP people are left in an age group, then the remaining doses for that day are given to the people of immediate lower age group and the process for that day continues until the vaccines for that day are finished or the entire population has been vaccinated. The selection of a person from a given age group is done randomly.

Given XXX, PPP, and Chef’s age group GGG, tell the minimum and the maximum number of days will it take for Chef to get vaccinated.

###Input


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, twelve integers GGG, PPP, X1,X2,…,X10X_1, X_2, \ldots, X_{10}X1​,X2​,…,X10​.
###Output
For each test case, output in a single line two space-separated integers, the minimum and maximum time required for Chef to get vaccinated.

###Constraints


1≤T≤1041 \leq T \leq 10^41≤T≤104
1≤G≤101 \leq G \leq 101≤G≤10
1≤P,Xi≤1051 \leq P, X_i \leq 10^51≤P,Xi​≤105
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a single line containing twelve integers: G P X1 X2 … X10. G (1‑10) is Chef's age‑group index, P is the number of vaccines administered per day, and Xi is the number of people in age‑group i.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output two space‑separated integers on a single line: the minimum possible day and the maximum possible day on which Chef can get vaccinated.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we compute higher = sum of people in groups older than G. Then minDay = floor(higher / P) + 1 and maxDay = ceil((higher + X_G) / P). The three lines match the calculations shown in the problem statement.

solution.approach: Vaccination proceeds strictly from the oldest age‑group to the youngest. Let higher = sum of Xi for groups i > G (people vaccinated before Chef's group). In each day exactly P people are vaccinated (or fewer on the last day). The first day on which Chef's group receives any dose is after floor(higher / P) full days, i.e. on day floor(higher / P) + 1. For the minimum possible day we assume Chef is among the first people vaccinated in his group, therefore the answer is minDay = floor(higher / P) + 1. For the maximum possible day we assume Chef is the last person of his group, so we need the day on which the cumulative number of vaccinated people reaches higher + XG. This is maxDay = ceil((higher + XG) / P). Both formulas are O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – only a constant amount of work per test case (summing at most 10 numbers).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        G = next(it)
        P = next(it)
        X = [0] + [next(it) for _ in range(10)]
        higher = sum(X[G+1:])
        min_day = higher // P + 1
        max_day = (higher + X[G] + P - 1) // P
        out_lines.append(f"{min_day} {max_day}")
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
